title: C ABI compatibility: extern fn pointers remain thin, using(X) extern fns are forbidden
description: Fat pointers break C ABI compatibility. extern fn pointers must remain thin (one word). Rule: a function declared extern cannot have a using() clause. A fat pointer cannot be passed to a C function expecting a fn pointer. The compiler emits an error at the boundary. Callers needing to pass allocating callbacks to C must write a thin wrapper that captures the allocator via a global or explicit parameter — same as today.
